chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Requires Full Blood Money and Half Blood Money in Cases Other Than the Taking of a Life

text: 5281 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ عَنْ بُرَيْدٍ اَلْعِجْلِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِنَّ فِي لِسَانِ اَلْأَخْرَسِ وَ عَيْنِ اَلْأَعْمَى وَ ذَكَرِ اَلْخَصِيِّ اَلْحُرِّ وَ أُنْثَيَيْهِ ثُلُثَ اَلدِّيَةِ وَ فِي ذَكَرِ اَلْغُلاَمِ اَلدِّيَةَ كَامِلَةً".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5281 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Abu Ayyub from Buraid Al-Ijli from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
"For the tongue of a mute person, the eye of a blind person, and the private parts and testicles of a free eunuch, one-third of the full blood money (diyya) is due.
However, for the private part of a young boy, the full blood money is due."
